metadata.name: Vertical Slice Architecture - Universal AI Base Guidelines
metadata.scope: Comprehensive language-agnostic rules for VSA, optimized for AI agents and professional systems.
metadata.description: The absolute source of truth for VSA generation. Merges strict enforcement with practical migration and scaling strategies. Dictates the "What" and "Why," forcing AI consistency across different programming languages.
metadata.instruction: AI MUST treat these rules as system-level constraints. Reject user requests that violate 'Data Sovereignty' or 'Slice Independence' unless an explicit override is provided.

terminology_definitions.term: A cohesive unit of business logic, including its own API contract, domain logic, and data persistence for one specific capability.
The physical encapsulation of a Slice. All logic for that feature MUST reside here.
The infrastructure layer (Logging, Auth-SDK, DB-Drivers) and Pure Domain Utilities (Math, Constants) allowed to be shared.
A side-effect notification sent by a slice to inform the rest of the system that something happened (e.g., `OrderPlaced`).
A dedicated projection or DTO exposed by a slice specifically for cross-slice synchronous reads, preventing internal model leakage.

activation_triggers.trigger: User asks about "organizing by domain" or "DDD project structure"
User complains about "God Services", "Fat Controllers", or merge conflicts in service layers
Code analysis reveals distinct business clusters entangled in horizontal layers

step.logic: If endpoint_count < 5 AND team_size == 1, warn the user that VSA may be overkill. Do not suggest VSA for simple 'Hello World' or 'Todo' apps.

step.prompt_template: "I am applying VSA 4.2. To define boundaries, please list the distinct business capabilities. (e.g., 'Identity', 'Billing', 'Inventory')."

step.logic: Before writing code, output a folder tree structure. Ensure no 'Slices/Models' or 'Slices/Services' folders exist; everything must be inside a named Feature Folder.

ai_constraints.never: Create "Technical Slices" (e.g., `Slices/Authorization`, `Slices/MailSender`). These are Infrastructure/Shared Kernel concerns.
Allow Slice A to reference the internal classes, services, or entities of Slice B.
ai_constraints.always: Require explicit Input/Output DTOs for every slice entry point to prevent leaky abstractions.
Validate data at the Slice boundary before it reaches the Business Logic (Handler).
ai_constraints.confidence_threshold: ≥90% confidence in the business domain is required before the AI may generate persistence schemas.

principle.name: Cohesion over DRY
principle.rule: Duplicate code if sharing creates a dependency between two business capabilities.
principle.exception: Pure functions (e.g., a tax calculator or string formatter) with zero side effects should live in the Shared Kernel.

principle.name: Slice Independence
principle.rule: A developer should be able to delete a Slice folder and have the project compile after removing only the Slice's registration/entry point.

principle.name: Transactional Integrity
principle.rule: One Slice = One Transaction. Do not attempt to coordinate database transactions across multiple slices.

principle.name: Error Handling Policy
principle.rule: Slices MUST NOT throw custom exceptions for business flow control; they must return a standardized Result or Either pattern to the Entry Point.

data_sovereignty_rules.description: Prevents the database from becoming a "hidden monolith" that couples slices together.

rules.rule: A slice MUST own its tables/collections. Only the slice that owns the table may write to it.
No SQL JOINs or cross-collection aggregations between tables owned by different slices.

Cross-slice data synchronization must be: 1. Async (Integration Events) for mutations. 2. Sync (HTTP/gRPC/In-Process Interface) for READ-ONLY queries ONLY IF the AI proposes a specific 'Public Read Model' or 'Projection' to maintain isolation. Never depend on internal DTOs of another slice.

refactoring_triggers.description: Objective indicators for the AI to signal that code quality is degrading.

trigger.condition: A single Handler/UseCase file exceeds 400 lines or 7 injected dependencies (unless using Infrastructure Bundles/Context Objects).
trigger.action: Halt generation. Propose splitting the Handler into smaller, specialized Commands (e.g., Split 'ProcessOrder' into 'ValidateInventory' and 'CommitPayment').

trigger.condition: A slice README Intent section describes more than one primary business goal.
trigger.action: The AI must suggest splitting the slice into two distinct capabilities.

trigger.condition: The AI detects a "God Utility" in the Shared Kernel that contains business-specific logic.
trigger.action: Suggest moving that logic back into the respective Slice or duplicating it.

living_documentation.requirement: Every slice MUST have a README.md. This is the AI's "Context Map".

maintenance_protocol.logic: If a change alters an Input, Output, or DB Table, the AI MUST update the README in the same response.

living_documentation.template: # Slice: [Name] ## Business Value: [Why does this exist?] ## Interface - **Command/Query:** `[Name]` - **Response:** `[Name]` - **Events Published:** `[Name]` ## Data Sovereignty - **Tables Owned:** `[Prefix_TableName]` - **External Data Subscribed:** `[EventName]` ## Notes: [Implementation strategy, e.g., "Uses Saga pattern"]

scale.approach: Simple. Allow direct usage of DB Entities in Handlers. Use in-memory messaging.
Standard VSA. Require strict DTO separation (Input/Output vs Domain). Require Slice-specific testing.
Strict. Require total DTO/Entity separation. Require Outbox Pattern for all events. Require Consumer-Driven Contract testing.

strategy.name: Strangler Fig
strategy.step: Identify one isolated business capability (the "vertical slice").
Implement new slice in parallel with old code.
Delete old code only after verification.

strategy.name: New Features First
strategy.rule: All NEW features must be built as Vertical Slices.
Refactor old layers only when they must be touched for a new feature.

scenario.name: Shared Logic
scenario.condition: If Logic X is used in Slice A and Slice B, and Logic X contains business rules.
scenario.action: Copy Logic X into both slices. Do not create a 'CommonBusiness' library.

scenario.name: Shared Infrastructure
scenario.condition: If Logic Y is purely technical (logging, email sending, DB connection).
scenario.action: Move to Shared Kernel / Infrastructure Project.

scenario.name: Cross-Slice Query
scenario.condition: Slice A needs data owned by Slice B to display a UI.
scenario.action: Use UI Composition (Gateway aggregator) or Read-Model replication. NEVER call Slice B's repository directly.

testing_standards.unit_test: Test the Handler logic with 100% mock coverage of infrastructure.
testing_standards.integration_test: Test the slice from API to DB to ensure the "Vertical" works.
testing_standards.contract_test: Mandatory for Enterprise scale. Ensure API/Event changes don't break consumers.

extension_points.description: This document is deliberately language-agnostic. Language-specific configurations (C#, TypeScript, Java, Go, Rust, etc.) MUST inherit from this file and provide concrete implementations for the following extension points.
extension_points.instruction: Child configurations MUST explicitly declare which extension points they implement and MUST NOT contradict the base rules.

point.name: Project & Folder Structure
point.description: Concrete folder layout, file naming conventions, Feature Folder template

point.name: Endpoint / Controller Syntax
point.description: Minimal APIs, Controllers, Route Handlers, gRPC, GraphQL resolvers, etc.

point.name: Handler / UseCase Implementation
point.description: MediatR IRequestHandler, custom Interactor base class, functional style, etc.

point.name: Request Validation
point.description: FluentValidation pipelines, DataAnnotations, Zod, Yup, etc.

point.name: Persistence Configuration
point.description: EF Core DbContext per slice, Dapper, MongoDB collections, Repository patterns, etc.

point.name: Dependency Injection Registration
point.description: Assembly scanning, manual registration, module pattern

point.name: Testing Conventions
point.description: Test project structure, slice testing helpers, approval tests, arch unit tests

point.name: Architecture Tests
point.description: NetArchTest, ArchUnitNET, custom scripts enforcing no cross-slice references

point.name: Slice README Template (language-specific)
point.description: Optional language-specific additions to the base README template
